claimText: The Biological Diversity (Access and Benefit Sharing) Regulation 2025 was notified by the National Biodiversity Authority (NBA) to replace the 2014 Guidelines.
evidenceExcerpt: Notified by the National Biodiversity Authority (NBA) to replace 2014 Guidelines.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the notifying authority for ABS regulations.
distractorHooks: Notified directly by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)

claimText: The 2025 ABS Regulations explicitly include Digital Sequence Information (DSI), which are digital representations of genetic resources like DNA/RNA, as a mode of benefit sharing.
evidenceExcerpt: Includes Digital Sequence Information (DSI) - digital representations of genetic resources like DNA/RNA.
examUse: Statement-based question on the scope of biological resources covered under the new ABS regulations.
distractorHooks: Applies only to physical biological samples

claimText: Access to cultivated medicinal plants notified under the Biological Diversity Act 2002 is exempted from the 2025 ABS Regulations.
evidenceExcerpt: Exempts access to cultivated medicinal plants notified under BDA 2002.
examUse: Statement-based question testing knowledge of exemptions under the ABS framework.
distractorHooks: All medicinal plants, including wild ones, are exempted
Cultivated medicinal plants are subject to the highest benefit-sharing slab

claimText: Under the 2025 ABS Regulations, entities with an annual turnover of up to 5 Crore are subject to a 'nil' benefit sharing slab.
evidenceExcerpt: Benefit sharing slabs range from nil (up to 5 Cr turnover) to 0.6% (above 250 Cr turnover).
examUse: Statement-based question on the financial thresholds and exemptions for benefit sharing.
distractorHooks: A flat benefit sharing rate applies to all entities regardless of turnover
Entities with turnover up to 50 Crore are exempted

claimText: High-Value Biological Resources, such as red sanders, require a minimum of 5% of auction or sale proceeds to be shared as benefits under the 2025 ABS Regulations.
evidenceExcerpt: High-Value Biological Resources (e.g., red sanders) require minimum 5% of auction/sale proceeds.
examUse: Statement-based question on specific provisions for high-value biological resources.
distractorHooks: Exempted from benefit sharing if auctioned by the government
